description:
You are an infrastructure engineer writing a tiny provisioning script.  
    Your goal is to prepare a miniature, **file-based** “service configuration” and record the steps you took.  
    The automated grader will validate the exact paths, names, contents and permissions you create, so please follow
    the specification literally.

    STEPS TO PERFORM
    1. Create a directory  /home/user/.config/exampled/        (0700 is fine; create parent directories if missing).  
    2. Inside that directory create a file named  cache-clean.conf   whose contents must be *exactly* (including case, spacing and blank lines):

[Service]
Name=cache-clean
Schedule=hourly
Command=rm -rf /home/user/cache/*


       • The file **must end with a single trailing newline** (Unix line-ending “\n”).  
       • No extra spaces before or after any line.

    3. Create a directory  /home/user/logs/   (0700 is fine; create if absent).  
    4. Inside that directory create a log file named  provisioning_steps.log   whose contents must be *exactly*:

created_dir
wrote_conf
verified_conf

       • Again, one trailing newline after the last line, no extra spaces.

    5. Finally, display the full contents of  /home/user/.config/exampled/cache-clean.conf   to STDOUT so the user (and the grader’s transcript) can see it.

    NOTES & HINTS
    • Normal user permissions are sufficient; do *not* use sudo.  
    • The whole job can be done with a small sequence of 3–4 shell commands (mkdir, printf/echo, cat, etc.).  
    • Make sure you actually write the log file in step 4; the grader will read it to confirm your actions.  
    • Don’t print anything other than the requested file contents in the final display.

    WHAT THE GRADER CHECKS
    • The existence of the two directories with correct names.  
    • The presence and *exact* byte-for-byte contents of the two files.  
    • That cache-clean.conf ends with a newline and has no superfluous blank lines.  
    • That provisioning_steps.log contains the three mandated lines in order, each ending with “\n”.  
    • That your terminal output includes the correct cache-clean.conf text.

    If everything matches, the task passes.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth used by the automated tests.  Do NOT reveal publicly. -->
    <file path="/home/user/.config/exampled/cache-clean.conf" mode="0644">
[Service]
Name=cache-clean
Schedule=hourly
Command=rm -rf /home/user/cache/*

</file>

    <file path="/home/user/logs/provisioning_steps.log" mode="0644">
created_dir
wrote_conf
verified_conf

</file>
